faqs.question: what is x402?
faqs.answer: x402 is the marketplace protocol where AI agents discover, pay for, and call tools using USDC micropayments on Solana. The HTTP 402 status from each tool prompts a payment + retry flow.

faqs.question: how do i publish a tool?
faqs.answer: expose any HTTP endpoint that returns 402 Payment Required when called without payment. Submit the URL + price + metadata via the publish form. Once approved, listed tools surface in the gallery and route through the gateway.

faqs.question: how do payments work?
faqs.answer: agents send USDC to your payTo address with a memo containing the tool ID. The gateway verifies the tx on devnet, attaches an X-Payment-Signature header, and forwards the call to your endpoint.

faqs.question: what are the rate limits?
faqs.answer: 60 requests/minute per caller wallet by default. Returning 429 surfaces the X-RateLimit-Reset and Retry-After headers so well-behaved clients self-throttle.

faqs.question: who can call my tool?
faqs.answer: any agent or human with a Solana wallet and devnet USDC. Set an allowlist via the publish form if you only want specific wallets.

faqs.question: where do i find error codes?
faqs.answer: see the gateway error code reference at /gateway/docs/error-codes.md — covers all 4xx and 5xx codes plus retry headers.

faqs.question: do you provide MCP clients?
faqs.answer: yes — node + python + bash references under /examples/. Each shows the full payment + invoke flow with rate-limit handling.

faqs.question: how do i track earnings?
faqs.answer: the /usage dashboard shows per-tool revenue, call volume, top callers, and a 12-month area chart. Hit /v1/tools/:id/stats for raw JSON.

faqs.question: when does mainnet launch?
faqs.answer: after a third-party audit. We're collecting waitlist signups on the publish page. Expect Q3 2026.
